mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, driving immediate positive price adjustments in the S&P 500 over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations, triggering negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in over 65 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicate a collapsing housing sector that drags down broader economic demand, causing negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2.0 percent year-over-year during late-cycle expansions confirms robust manufacturing output that supports corporate earnings and drives positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth during late-cycle phases yields negative or flat 20-day forward returns in over 60 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates falling below 0.5 percent indicate extreme monetary accommodation that fuels risk-on sentiment and liquidity-driven equity rallies over the following week.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain negative or flat in 70 percent of periods when FEDFUNDS drops below 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and generates positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Concurrent PAYEMS increases and UNRATE decreases fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with Rank IC below 0.04 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations and profit margins, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 prices over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across all tested samples.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production indices dropping below the 100 baseline level signal severe supply-side contraction that forces earnings downgrades and triggers sharp negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in over 60 percent of instances when INDPRO falls below 100.
